title: Introduction & Job Market Research
subhead: Welcome to the Skarion Accounting Track. Let's explore what the job market actually demands.

chunks.title: The Skarion Approach
chunks.bodyHtml:
Most accounting courses teach you how to pass the CPA exam. We teach you how to do the actual job of a Staff Accountant.

You will step into the role of a newly hired accountant at Skarion Manufacturing LLC. By the end of this track, you will have reconciled actual bank statements, processed messy payroll, and completed a full month-end close.

steps.title: Daily
steps.desc: Process vendor bills, record customer payments, and categorize bank feed transactions.

steps.title: Weekly
steps.desc: Run payroll, issue customer invoices, and review Accounts Receivable aging.

steps.title: Month-End
steps.desc: Reconcile bank accounts, post accrual journal entries, and generate financial statements.

chunks.title: What Employers Actually Want
chunks.bodyHtml:
We analyzed over 40 real-world job postings for Entry-Level and Staff Accountants. The results might surprise you.

  • Excel is listed more often than QuickBooks.
  • Month-end close experience is the #1 differentiator.
  • Employers expect you to understand Accrual Accounting, not just basic bookkeeping.

callout.html: Don't just list 'Excel' on your resume. List 'VLOOKUP, PivotTables, and Bank Reconciliation in Excel'.

questions.q: Based on market research, which software skill is most frequently explicitly required for Staff Accountants?
questions.explanation: Excel is the undisputed king. Every company uses it, even if they have an ERP like SAP or NetSuite.
